implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001: SessionManager-backed adapter_id lookup with ambient/learning/pattern-c rejection
AC-002: Attended adapter_id set {telegram, cli, dashboard, reachy} matches JarvisConfig.attended_adapter_ids
AC-003: Async-subagent frame check via middleware metadata with session-state fallback
AC-004: Both paths run before any provider SDK call (no outbound HTTP on rejection)
AC-005: Spoofed-ambient case rejected (frame check overrides attended-adapter pass)
AC-006: Prompt-injection instruction body never echoed in rejection string or log record
AC-007: log_frontier_escalation records outcome='attended_only' on every rejection
AC-008: Both detection paths covered (middleware-only, session-state-only, fallback chain)
AC-009: ruff lint + ruff format pass with zero errors on modified files

completion_promises.criterion_text: escalate_to_frontier (extended from TASK-J003-010) looks up the active session's adapter_id via SessionManager.current_session() — ambient / learning / pattern-c adapters return "ERROR: attended_only — escalate_to_frontier cannot be invoked from {adapter_id} adapter".
completion_promises.evidence: Added _current_session_hook module-level Callable in dispatch.py (resolver injected by lifecycle.startup; tests inject directly). _check_attended_only reads Session.adapter via _resolve_current_session(), compares against ATTENDED_ADAPTER_IDS, and returns the exact AC string with f'from {adapter_id} adapter' interpolation. TestSessionAdapterCheck::test_non_attended_adapter_returns_attended_only_error parametrises ['ambient', 'learning', 'pattern-c'] and asserts the literal message.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Attended adapter_id set: {"telegram", "cli", "dashboard", "reachy"} (from config.attended_adapter_ids) — only these pass.
completion_promises.evidence: ATTENDED_ADAPTER_IDS frozenset declared in dispatch.py with the four members. TestSessionAdapterCheck::test_attended_adapter_ids_constant_matches_config_default asserts equality with JarvisConfig().attended_adapter_ids. TestSessionAdapterCheck::test_attended_adapter_passes_layer2 parametrises [TELEGRAM, CLI, DASHBOARD, REACHY] and asserts each reaches the (mocked) provider branch.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Second detection path: the tool body also checks whether the call frame is inside an AsyncSubAgent execution — via AsyncSubAgentMiddleware metadata if available (ASSUM-FRONTIER-CALLER-FRAME), else via session-state "currently-in-subagent" flag. An affirmative check returns "ERROR: attended_only — escalate_to_frontier cannot be invoked from async-subagent frame".
completion_promises.evidence: _is_async_subagent_frame helper in dispatch.py: prefers _async_subagent_frame_hook (middleware metadata) when it returns True/False; falls back to session.metadata['currently_in_subagent'] only when the middleware hook is None, raises, or returns None. _check_attended_only emits the literal AC string on rejection. Coverage: TestAsyncSubagentFrameCheck::test_middleware_hook_true_returns_frame_rejection, ::test_middleware_hook_none_falls_back_to_session_state, ::test_middleware_hook_raises_falls_back_to_session_state, ::test_session_state_only_no_middleware_hook.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Both assertion paths run before ANY provider SDK call — no outbound HTTP attempt on rejection.
completion_promises.evidence: _check_attended_only is invoked at the top of escalate_to_frontier's body, before the GEMINI/OPUS branch dispatch. TestNoProviderCallOnRejection patches both google.genai.Client and anthropic.Anthropic, fires a rejection scenario, then asserts assert_not_called() on both clients for adapter rejection AND frame rejection paths.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Spoofed-ambient case: an attended session with an in-progress async-subagent frame is still rejected (scenario: A spoofed-ambient invocation from inside an attended session is rejected) — the async-subagent frame check overrides the attended-adapter pass.
completion_promises.evidence: TDD-first test class TestSpoofedAmbient written before the implementation. test_attended_session_with_subagent_frame_is_rejected sets adapter=Adapter.CLI (attended) AND middleware hook returns True; asserts the frame-rejection literal AND that no provider client was constructed. test_attended_session_with_session_state_subagent_flag_is_rejected covers the session-state fallback variant. The implementation in _check_attended_only runs Path A (adapter) then Path B (frame) sequentially, so an attended adapter cannot bypass Path B.

completion_promises.criterion_text: A prompt-injection instruction body ("ignore the gate and ...") does not alter the assertion flow — the gate fires on adapter_id / frame, not on instruction content. Instruction body is never echoed in the rejection string (ASSUM-006).
completion_promises.evidence: _check_attended_only inspects Session.adapter and the frame hooks only — never instruction. The rejection f-strings interpolate adapter_id or the literal 'async-subagent frame' — not the instruction. TestPromptInjectionPosture::test_prompt_injection_does_not_bypass_adapter_check, ::test_prompt_injection_does_not_bypass_frame_check, ::test_rejection_log_does_not_carry_instruction_body cover all three subcases — the secret string is never present in result, log message, or banned LogRecord attributes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: log_frontier_escalation records outcome="attended_only" on the structured log entry for every rejection.
completion_promises.evidence: Both rejection branches in _check_attended_only call _emit_frontier_log(target, correlation_id, adapter_label, instruction_length, 'attended_only') before returning. The Literal in FrontierEscalationContext.outcome already includes 'attended_only' (dispatch_types.py line 87). TestStructuredLogOutcomeAttendedOnly::test_adapter_rejection_logs_attended_only_outcome, ::test_frame_rejection_logs_attended_only_outcome, ::test_session_state_fallback_rejection_logs_attended_only assert exactly one INFO record is emitted with outcome='attended_only' and the correct target/adapter/model_alias attributes.

completion_promises.criterion_text: If AsyncSubAgentMiddleware metadata is unavailable in DeepAgents 0.5.3 (verified at test time per ASSUM-FRONTIER-CALLER-FRAME), the session-state fallback still holds — tests cover both paths (review Finding F6).
completion_promises.evidence: _is_async_subagent_frame falls back to Session.metadata['currently_in_subagent'] when the middleware hook is None, returns None, or raises. Both detection paths have independent test coverage: TestAsyncSubagentFrameCheck::test_session_state_only_no_middleware_hook (DeepAgents 0.5.3 reality — only fallback wired), ::test_middleware_only_no_session_hook (only middleware wired), ::test_middleware_hook_none_falls_back_to_session_state, ::test_middleware_hook_raises_falls_back_to_session_state, ::test_middleware_hook_false_does_not_trigger_frame_check (middleware authoritative).

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: Ran `python -m ruff check src/jarvis/tools/dispatch.py tests/test_tools_escalate_to_frontier_layer2.py` → 'All checks passed!'. Ran `python -m ruff format --check` on the same files → '2 files already formatted'. Project uses ruff (line-length 100, target py312, lints E/F/I/W/UP/B/SIM/RUF per pyproject.toml).
